text: Beewɩya Gyɔɔŋ Wɩɩsɩnɩɩfool hʋ ko daga ma wɩtɩɩ ŋmanɩɩ hʋ rɛ ma aa sɩ tɩŋa, aŋka ma bɩ laa ʋ wɩya di, amɛ lampolaala abee gyaŋtɩrɩsɩ kɛ laa ʋ wɩya di rɛ. Abee ma aa naa gɛɛ hʋ ba aa laa ʋ wɩya di buloŋ, amɛ ma kɛ bɩ bɩrɩma ma hakɩllɩ a laa ʋ wɩya di.” Yesu maga baga tʋŋtʋnna badɔmɔŋ namaga Maakɩ

I couldn't find any information on the Paasaal language, and it's possible that it's a language that is not widely spoken or documented. However, I can try to provide a translation based on my understanding of the text. Please note that this translation may not be accurate.

Here is my attempt at translating the text:

"[The kingdom of heaven is like a master who gave a task to his servants and went on a journey, and he gave them talents, five to one, two to another, and one to another, according to their abilities. The one who received five talents went and traded with them and gained five more. The one who received two talents also gained two more. But the one who received one talent went and dug a hole and hid his master's money. The master of those servants came and settled accounts with them." Jesus told his disciples this parable.

ref: and he said to them, “I am so sad that I feel as if I am dying. Stay here and keep awake with me.”
text: Nyɛ rɛ ʋ basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Ŋ tɩya buloŋ nɛ kyogo ŋ bembii kɩ kyɛ dɩ ʋ bori. Ma hɔŋ daha kɩ gyegili ŋ?”
translated: The rabbit said to his sister, "My sister, what is the reason you are always running away from me? Am I a monster?"

ref: But I tell you that Solomon with all his wealth wasn't as well clothed as one of them.
text: amɛ ba weliye rɛ kɩŋkaŋ. Kuwori Solomɔn gyɩ kaŋ kɩna rɛ weliŋ. Amɛ abee gɛɛ buloŋ ʋ gannɩ fa bɩ weliye a maga tɩɩfɩɩla no kɩdɩgɩ buloŋ.
translated: They said the elephant is big. King Solomon's wisdom is bigger than the elephant. They asked for a rope to tie the elephant and a big stick to hit the elephant on the back.

ref: Don't worry about tomorrow. It will take care of itself. You have enough to worry about today.
text: Ɛɛwɩya, ma ta leŋ dɩ kyiye wɩya kyɩl ma. Kyɛɛ buloŋ kaŋ ʋ wɩgyʋʋlɩya rɛ. Ma ta bɩl kpaa wɩdʋma buloŋ pɛ kyɛɛ kɩdɩgɩ buloŋ bee ʋ wɩgyʋʋlɩya tɩyaŋ.”
translated: They said, I should take the book and read it. But the book was written in a language I didn't understand. I had to look for someone who could interpret the book for me so that I could understand what was written in it.

ref: But after the crowd had been sent out of the house, Jesus went to the girl's bedside. He took her by the hand and helped her up.
text: Amɛ ba aa kile nala hʋ buloŋ ba lɩɩ gɛɛ, Yesu mʋ gyʋʋ haŋtolibiye hʋ dɩya, a mʋ kaŋ ʋ noŋ tɩyaŋ, ʋ sii.
translated: My father and his family were all in the house, Jesus came quietly to the door, my father got up to open it, he did.
